title: VOO ETF Review (2026): Vanguard S&P 500 Fees & Performance
description: Complete 2026 review of the Vanguard S&P 500 ETF (VOO). Discover its true expense ratio, holdings, performance, and how it compares to SPY and IVV.

mainEntity.name: What is the VOO expense ratio?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Vanguard S&P 500 ETF (VOO) has an incredibly low expense ratio of 0.03%. This means for every $10,000 invested, you pay only $3 per year in management fees.

mainEntity.name: VOO vs SPY: Which is better?

acceptedAnswer.text: For long-term, buy-and-hold investors, VOO is generally considered better than SPY because its expense ratio is lower (0.03% vs 0.09%). SPY is more popular for active traders due to higher liquidity and options volume.
Yes, VOO pays a quarterly dividend. The dividend yield fluctuates with the market, typically tracking the S&P 500 average.
VOO holds approximately 500 of the largest U.S. companies. The top holdings are heavily weighted toward massive tech companies like Apple, Microsoft, NVIDIA, Amazon, and Alphabet.

mainEntity.name: Is VOO a good investment for beginners?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, VOO is widely considered an excellent core holding for beginners because it provides instant diversification across 500 leading U.S. companies at an extremely low cost.
